title: Viola × balearica
taxonomy: Viola × balearica is a hybrid species within the genus Viola. It is native to the Balearic Islands, specifically found in the wild on the island of Mallorca. The classification of Viola × balearica is as a hybrid of the species Viola reichenbachiana and Viola riviniana. The exact classification can also be represented as a subspecies of Viola reichenbachiana, with the specific classification being Viola reichenbachiana subsp. balearica. This subspecies is endemic to the Balearic Islands and has distinct characteristics that differentiate it from the parent species.
uses: Viola × balearica has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the Balearic Islands, where it is native. Its leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds. In some traditional remedies, it is used to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Viola × balearica for medicinal use.
benefits: Viola × balearica, also known as the Balearic Violet, is said to have various health benefits due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. It has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve skin health. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal remedies, and it is also consumed as a tea. Some studies suggest that Viola × balearica may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could help to prevent the onset of certain diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.

constituents: Viola × balearica contains active constituents such as flavonoids, anthocyanins, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Viola × balearica are also known to have a potential role in protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's saponins have been found to possess immunomodulatory and anti-cancer properties. preparations: Viola × balearica is used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. side_effects: Viola × balearica is not typically considered for its medicinal properties, but due to its potential lookalike plants with different side effects, proper identification is crucial.  However, some Viola species may cause allergic reactions in certain individuals. If ingested, Viola plants may cause gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and vomiting.
